---
name: threenative-workflow
description: Use the generated ThreeNative project workflow from Codex. Applies when editing structured source, adding gameplay, inspecting scenes, or verifying a generated ThreeNative project.
---

# ThreeNative Generated Project Workflow

Use `AGENTS.md` as the authoritative local instructions.

Default loop:

```bash
pnpm tn -- game plan --goal "<game idea>" --project . --json
pnpm tn -- cookbook list --json
pnpm tn -- project map --project . --json
pnpm tn -- iterate --project . --json
```

Prefer bounded source operations:

- `pnpm tn -- scene inspect <scene-id> --node <id> --project . --json`
- `pnpm tn -- add <mechanic-block> ... --project . --json`
- `pnpm tn -- material ... --project . --json`
- `pnpm tn -- ui ... --project . --json`

Do not use standalone validate/build/playtest as the normal verification loop.
If `tn iterate` reports a playtest issue, use:

```bash
pnpm tn -- playtest report --latest --scenario <name> --json
```

Keep durable edits in `content/**/*.json` and `src/scripts/**/*.ts`; do not edit
`dist/**`, emitted bundle JSON, or `scripts.bundle.js`.

item.text: I’m gathering the local contract and starter files now: the typed spec, API card, and package scripts will determine the narrow compile and verification path.

item.aggregated_output:
# CLAUDE.md

Use `AGENTS.md` as the authoritative local instructions.

In short: prefer `tn ... --json` for authoring, especially `tn scene`,
`tn ui`, `tn prefab set-material`, and `tn add` mechanic-block commands. Edit
durable source under `content/**` and `src/scripts/**` only when no command
covers the change, and do not repair generated bundle files.
Use `tn scene inspect arena --node <id> --project . --json` for one entity,
resource, system, prefab, or UI node before opening the full scene JSON.
For gameplay verification, run `tn iterate --project . --json` first; do not
run validate/build/playtest separately unless its compact diagnostic asks for
deeper proof.

# Agent Game Plan

Complete this worksheet before creating or substantially changing game source.
Use it as the implementation checklist, and keep
`artifacts/game-production/plan.json` as the machine-readable plan evidence.

## Game Goal

- User request:
- Template:
- Project path:
- Date:
- Planned game category for asset catalog search:

## First Commands

Run these before mutating `content/**/*.json` or `src/scripts/**/*.ts`:

```bash
tn game inspect --project . --json
tn game plan --goal "<game idea>" --project . --apply --json
pnpm run game:plan
```

Use `--apply` only when the goal matches a supported scaffold-first category
such as top-down collector or lane runner. Omit `--apply` for non-mutating
planning or unsupported genres.

If a command is unavailable, record the diagnostic code, path, severity, and
message here before choosing a fallback.

## Playable Loop

- Player verb:
- Controls:
- Objective:
- Progression:
- Fail/retry path:
- Scoring or persistent state:
- Feedback moments:

## High-Value Surface Inventory

Plan every high-value surface before source mutation.

| Surface | Source owner | Asset/source plan | Fallback blocker |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Player/hero |  |  |  |
| Obstacle/enemy/vehicle |  |  |  |
| Reward/interactable |  |  |  |
| World/environment |  |  |  |
| UI/HUD |  |  |  |
| Audio feedback |  |  |  |

Primitive geometry is the last fallback. Do not mark primitive-only or
primitive-looking high-value surfaces as finished.

## UI Approach

- native ThreeNative UI is the portable default for HUD, prompts, menus,
  retained UI state, and UI that coordinates with 3D/game state.
- React webview UI is an optional screen-space panel layer for inventories,
  settings, shops, maps, dialogs, and similar overlays.
- Webview UI cannot attach to a 3D element and must not become the source of
  portable gameplay state.

## Asset Sourcing Plan

For each 3D model surface, search the shipped SQLite asset catalog first:

```bash
tn asset source search --game-category <category> --format glb --direct-only --json
tn asset source get <asset-source-id> --json
tn asset inspect assets --recursive --json
tn model-test assets/<model>.glb --json
```

Record selected catalog records next to committed assets.

| Surface | Search command | asset-source-id | Source URL | Provenance URL | Origin | License evidence | Review status | Downloaded date | Conversion notes | Fallback |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Player/hero |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Obstacle/enemy/vehicle |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Reward/interactable |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| World/environment |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

Only after catalog search fails should you check curated open-source packs,
compatible GitHub/open-source packs, authored custom meshes, and finally
primitive fallback geometry.

## Animation And Scale Plan

- Active actor clips to inspect:
- Intended idle/run/action clips:
- Runtime bounds expectations for hero:
- Runtime bounds expectations for vehicles, obstacles, rewards, landmarks, and
  environment:
- Camera, pose, lighting, and speed alternatives to incoherent scale changes:

```bash
tn game scale --project . --json
```

## Source Ownership

Name the durable source owner for every planned behavior.

| Behavior or state | content/**/*.json owner | src/scripts/**/*.ts module | Export | Proof |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Player control |  |  |  |  |
| Objective/progression |  |  |  |  |
| Fail/retry |  |  |  |  |
| HUD/state binding |  |  |  |  |
| Audio/VFX feedback |  |  |  |  |

Do not author raw Three.js scenes, raw Bevy/Rust gameplay, DOM gameplay,
filesystem access, workers, timers, renderer handles, or native runtime
handles.

## Polish Checklist

- [ ] Player/hero silhouette is recognizable and not placeholder geometry.
- [ ] Obstacle/enemy/vehicle silhouette is readable.
- [ ] Reward/interactable is easy to identify.
- [ ] World/environment has context, boundaries, landmarks, and scale cues.
- [ ] Materials communicate surface type through color, roughness/metalness,
      texture/normal detail where available, and coherent UV scale.
- [ ] Lighting and camera framing make the objective readable.
- [ ] Set dressing supports gameplay without obscuring it.
- [ ] VFX, motion, and audio feedback communicate state changes.
- [ ] UI states cover gameplay, pause, settings, loading, fail/retry,
      win/milestone, and touch controls when applicable.
- [ ] Mobile fit is checked.
- [ ] Performance budget and asset counts are recorded.

## Proof Checklist

Run the narrowest relevant proof first, then finish with the production loop.
Use `tn playtest` as an edit loop, not only as a final gate: after each
gameplay/input/script change, run the focused playtest, inspect the compact
stdout or `tn playtest report --latest --scenario <name> --json`, repair the
owning `content/**/*.json` or `src/scripts/**/*.ts`, and rerun until the proof
passes. Open deep machine logs such as `effect-log.json`, `observations.json`,
`runtime-trace.json`, `console.json`, or `network.json` only when the compact
report points to a specific diagnostic that requires them.

Discover what is provable before writing a scenario:

```bash
tn playtest --project . --discover --json
tn playtest --project . --suggest-scenario smoke-movement --json
```

For multi-step mechanics, create a committed scenario under
`playtests/*.playtest.json` and run it with stable artifacts. Use `--watch`
while iterating, and rerun with `--target desktop` to prove the native
runtime, not only web:

```bash
tn playtest --project . --scenario playtests/smoke-movement.playtest.json --stable-artifacts --json
tn playtest --project . --scenario playtests/smoke-movement.playtest.json --watch --pass-once --json
tn playtest --project . --scenario playtests/smoke-movement.playtest.json --target desktop --json
```

For a one-input smoke proof, use the one-shot form, then finish with the
production loop:

```bash
tn authoring validate --project . --json
tn build --project . --json
tn scene inspect <scene-id> --project . --json
tn playtest --project . --entity <entity-id> --press KeyD --frames 30 --expect-moved --json
tn screenshot --project . --url <preview-url> --out artifacts/game-production/screenshot.png --wait-ready --json
tn game scale --project . --json
tn game score --project . --json
tn game qa --project . --run-proof --json
tn game release --project . --json
```

- Build proof:
- Runtime readiness proof:
- Nonblank screenshot proof:
- Visible motion proof:
- Active character/vehicle animation proof:
- Scale proof:
- Input playtest proof:
- Score/QA/release blockers:
- Fallback evidence:

{
  "name": "threenative-structured-source-starter",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "tn": "node bin/tn",
    "build": "tn authoring compile-typed-spec --json && tn build",
    "dev:web": "tn dev --target web",
    "iterate": "tn iterate --project . --json",
    "playtest": "tn playtest --scenario playtests/smoke-movement.playtest.json --stable-artifacts --json",
    "playtest:archetype": "tn playtest --scenario playtests/smoke-movement.playtest.json --stable-artifacts --json",
    "playtest:native": "tn playtest --scenario playtests/native-smoke-movement.playtest.json --stable-artifacts --json",
    "game:plan": "tn game plan --goal \"small arena collectible game\" --project . --apply --json",
    "game:improve": "tn game improve --apply-plan artifacts/game-production/plan.json --project . --json",
    "game:score": "tn game score --project . --json",
    "game:qa": "tn game qa --project . --run-proof --json",
    "game:release": "tn game release --project . --json",
    "recipe:controller": "tn recipe third-person-controller --scene arena --entity player --camera camera.main --dry-run --json",
    "validate": "tn authoring compile-typed-spec --json && tn validate",
    "validate:authoring": "tn authoring compile-typed-spec --json && tn authoring validate --json",
    "verify": "tn verify --frames 2 --json",
    "authoring:compile": "tn authoring compile-typed-spec --json"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@threenative/script-stdlib": "file:/home/joao/projects/threejs-to-bevy/packages/script-stdlib",

  },

    "typescript": "^5.9.3"
  }
}

item.text: The project already has generated content files, but the benchmark condition requires the durable edit to start in `src/game.spec.ts`. I’m going to shape the typed spec to emit the arena, five pickups, HUD text, and game-state fields, then compile it before iterating.

The existing compiled content and typed spec are slightly out of sync, which is expected in this benchmark setup. I’m checking the compiler’s emitted schema support so the spec change lands cleanly instead of relying on unsupported fields.

item.text: I found the compiler and SDK source in the parent repo, which is allowed as read-only context; I’m keeping writes inside the candidate project. Next I’ll patch the spec and script with the smallest compatible surface.

I’m going to use script-owned resource state for collection, win, and retry. The typed spec will declare `GameState`, visible pickup entities, boundary/floor markers, and the script system so the compiled content owns the behavior contract.

---
id: hud-score-binding
goal: Bind a retained HUD text node to a source-owned score resource.
category: ui
scriptPath: src/scripts/player.ts
surfaces:
  - hud
  - resource
---

## commands
```bash
tn ui add-text hud score --text Score --project . --json
tn ui bind hud score --resource GameState.score --project . --json
```

## source-delta
```json
{"content/ui/hud.ui.json":"score text node binds to GameState.score."}
```

## script
```ts
import { Vector3, type ScriptContext } from "@threenative/script-stdlib";

export function movePlayerToGoal(context: ScriptContext): void {
  for (const entity of context.query()) {
    const transform = entity.transform();
    transform.position = Vector3.add(transform.position, [context.input.getAxis("MoveX") * context.time.fixedDelta * 2.4, 0, 0]);
  }
  context.resources.set?.("GameState", { countdown: "Score", score: 0 });
}
```

## proof
```bash
tn playtest --project . --scenario playtests/hud-resource.playtest.json --stable-artifacts --json
```

item.text: The built-in collector scaffold script confirms `context.state("GameState", ...)` and moving collected pickups out of view are supported patterns. I’ll implement the same idea, with a typed-spec-owned scene and a stronger retry/state loop.
Before editing, I’m checking the generated materials document so the typed spec doesn’t create duplicate IDs that validation rejects.

I’m editing `src/game.spec.ts` to define the collector scene, then `src/scripts/player.ts` for movement, collection, HUD state, win, and retry. These are durable authoring/script changes, not generated output edits.
The durable typed spec and script are updated. I’m compiling the typed spec now so generated structured source reflects those changes before running the normal iteration loop.

item.text: The package script uses a bare `tn` binary and fails because this isolated candidate has no `node_modules/.bin`. I’m using the documented fallback `pnpm tn -- authoring compile-typed-spec --project . --json`, which goes through the local `bin/tn` wrapper.
Typed-spec compilation succeeded and wrote scene/input/material content. I’m running the generated iteration loop now; if it reports a compact diagnostic, I’ll fix the owning source and rerun.

`iterate` found the remaining legacy systems document still references `movePlayerToGoal`. The typed spec compiled its scene-local system, but this project also validates `content/systems/arena.systems.json`, so I’m aligning that durable systems document to the new export and resource declarations.
The systems document now points at `updateCollector` with `GameState` read/write ownership. I’m rerunning `iterate` to check validation, build, screenshot, and the committed playtest.
`iterate` got through authoring validation and then rejected legacy `positionOr` usage. I’m switching the script to the current convention, `entity.transform().position`, and rerunning the loop.

The compiler treats `systems[].writes` as component names, so my typed spec was too literal by listing entity IDs. I’m correcting the spec to declare `Transform` writes only, then recompiling so the generated scene is fixed.

item.text: The report says the screenshot file is nonblank but there is no canvas/runtime-ready marker, which usually means the preview served an HTML error or diagnostic page. I’m opening the screenshot and bundle metadata to identify the visible failure.

The screenshot is just the dark page background; the plan also warned there was no camera. I’m adding a portable camera and light to the typed spec so the runtime can frame the arena, then I’ll recompile and rerun `iterate`.
The compiled scene has no camera. The bundled world had fallback lights from other source docs, but no active camera entity, so I’m adding `camera.main` and explicit lights in the typed spec itself.
The typed spec now owns the camera and lights. I’m recompiling and rerunning the full local iterate loop.

item.text: `iterate` passed all steps. I’m doing a quick visual check of the generated screenshot to catch obvious readability issues before closing.

The game passes, but the screenshot still shows the old `Ready` HUD from `content/ui/hud.ui.json`, and it occludes the player. I’m updating that retained HUD document to match the typed-spec score/status/retry bindings, then I’ll rerun `iterate`.
I’m replacing the old single `countdown` HUD node with three unobtrusive top-left text nodes bound to `GameState.scoreText`, `statusText`, and `retryText`, matching the gameplay state.
The HUD source now reflects score/progress and retry text. I’m rerunning `iterate` to verify the build and playtest after that UI change.
The verification loop is still green after the HUD fix. I’m checking the updated screenshot once more for readability.

The screenshot now meets the visual bar: visible character, five pickups, arena boundary, and score/progress HUD. I’m checking the existing playtest format in case a short collection scenario can verify the win/retry path without leaving the generated workflow.

The built-in movement playtest passed. To cover the benchmark’s objective more directly, I’m adding a focused `collect-all` playtest scenario and updating the stale HUD-resource scenario to the new HUD IDs, then I’ll run the scenario through `tn iterate`.

The collection scenario is committed under `playtests/collect-all.playtest.json`. I’m running it through `iterate` so validation, build, screenshot, and this focused playtest stay in the project loop.
The focused iterate run is still executing, likely because the collect-all scenario has a longer input path than the smoke test. I’m waiting for its result before making any further changes.

The focused scenario reached runtime but only failed the expected final score assertion. I’m inspecting its after screenshot and resource observations to see whether the path missed a pickup or the assertion shape is wrong.

item.text: The collect-all path moved the player across the arena but only collected one pickup; the pickup radius was too tight for the fixed-step input path. I’m making collection a bit more forgiving so the browser controls feel playable, then rerunning the focused iterate scenario.
The collection radius is now tolerant enough for keyboard movement through the pickup lanes. I’m rerunning the focused scenario through `iterate`.
The focused scenario is still running; I’m waiting on the final assertion result.
The focused collect-all scenario now passes through `iterate`. I’m checking the after screenshot so the final state is visibly the win state with the updated score.

The win state is proven, but the after screenshot still shows two collected pickups rendered below the arena because I only moved them downward. I’m switching collected pickups to `MeshRenderer.visible = false` and restoring visibility on retry, then rerunning the focused loop.
Collected pickups now hide through the renderer component instead of relying on position. I’m rerunning the collect-all iterate scenario to keep proof current.
The scenario is still running; I’m waiting for the final pass/fail output.
The renderer patch changed the scenario result, so I’m inspecting the actual final score and console diagnostics before deciding whether to keep component patching or use a different hide strategy.

item.text: The runtime diagnostic is clear: the system patches `MeshRenderer` but only declared `Transform`. I’m adding `MeshRenderer` to the typed spec and systems document writes, recompiling, and rerunning the focused scenario.
The system write declarations now include both `Transform` and `MeshRenderer`. I’m recompiling typed spec and rerunning the collect-all iterate scenario.

item.command: /usr/bin/zsh -lc 'pnpm tn -- authoring compile-typed-spec --project . --json && pnpm tn -- iterate --project . --scenario playtests/collect-all.playtest.json --json'

item.text: The focused loop is running again after the declaration fix; I’m waiting for completion.

item.text: The collect-all scenario is green again. I’m checking the final after screenshot to confirm collected pickups are no longer visibly lingering.
The final playtest screenshot shows `Score 5 / 5`, the win message, and no remaining pickups. I’m doing a short change/verification summary check now so the final response can be precise.

item.text:
Implemented the typed-spec collector benchmark in the candidate project.

Changed:
- [src/game.spec.ts](/home/joao/projects/threejs-to-bevy/tools/verify/artifacts/agent-benchmark/typed-spec-trial-2026-07-07a/candidates/collector-typed-spec-r1/src/game.spec.ts): arena, camera/lights, visible player, five pickups, HUD state, retry input, collector system.
- [src/scripts/player.ts](/home/joao/projects/threejs-to-bevy/tools/verify/artifacts/agent-benchmark/typed-spec-trial-2026-07-07a/candidates/collector-typed-spec-r1/src/scripts/player.ts): WASD/arrow movement, pickup collection, score updates, win state, retry reset.
- [content/ui/hud.ui.json](/home/joao/projects/threejs-to-bevy/tools/verify/artifacts/agent-benchmark/typed-spec-trial-2026-07-07a/candidates/collector-typed-spec-r1/content/ui/hud.ui.json): readable score/status/retry HUD.
- Added [playtests/collect-all.playtest.json](/home/joao/projects/threejs-to-bevy/tools/verify/artifacts/agent-benchmark/typed-spec-trial-2026-07-07a/candidates/collector-typed-spec-r1/playtests/collect-all.playtest.json).

Verification:
- Required plan command ran.
- `pnpm run authoring:compile` failed because the package script calls bare `tn` and this candidate has no `node_modules/.bin`; used the documented local wrapper instead.
- `pnpm tn -- authoring compile-typed-spec --project . --json` passed.
- `pnpm tn -- iterate --project . --scenario playtests/collect-all.playtest.json --json` passed.

Evidence:
- Report: [artifacts/iterate/latest/report.json](/home/joao/projects/threejs-to-bevy/tools/verify/artifacts/agent-benchmark/typed-spec-trial-2026-07-07a/candidates/collector-typed-spec-r1/artifacts/iterate/latest/report.json)
- Win screenshot: [artifacts/iterate/latest/playtest/after.png](/home/joao/projects/threejs-to-bevy/tools/verify/artifacts/agent-benchmark/typed-spec-trial-2026-07-07a/candidates/collector-typed-spec-r1/artifacts/iterate/latest/playtest/after.png)
